sections.heading: What is a GFR Calculator?
sections.content: A GFR (Glomerular Filtration Rate) Calculator estimates how well your kidneys are filtering blood. It uses your age, sex, and serum creatinine levels to provide an estimated GFR (eGFR) and identify your CKD (Chronic Kidney Disease) stage. This tool is essential for monitoring kidney health in both adults and children.

subSections.heading: Inputs Required
subSections.content: Age, Sex, Serum Creatinine (mg/dL or μmol/L), Pediatric or Adult selection.

subSections.heading: Formulas Used
subSections.content: CKD-EPI (most accurate for adults), MDRD (commonly used in CKD monitoring), and Schwartz Formula (for pediatric GFR calculation).

subSections.heading: What Happens Next
subSections.content: After entering details, the calculator estimates your eGFR and identifies CKD stage if applicable.

subSections.heading: Clear Kidney Health Insights
subSections.content: Quickly understand how efficiently your kidneys filter blood.

subSections.heading: CKD Monitoring
subSections.content: Track progression or detect early-stage kidney disease.

subSections.heading: Personalized Tracking
subSections.content: Separate calculators for adults and children ensure accuracy.

subSections.heading: Formula Comparison
subSections.content: Check results with CKD-EPI vs MDRD for optimal accuracy.

subSections.heading: Convenience
subSections.content: Fast, free, and online without manual calculations.

sections.heading: Tips to Improve Kidney Function Naturally

subSections.heading: Stay Hydrated
subSections.content: Drink enough water daily to support kidney filtration.

subSections.heading: Eat a Kidney-Friendly Diet
subSections.content: Limit salt, processed foods, and excess protein intake.

subSections.heading: Avoid Nephrotoxic Medications
subSections.content: Only take medications that affect kidneys if prescribed.

subSections.heading: Exercise Regularly
subSections.content: Supports cardiovascular health, indirectly improving kidneys.

subSections.heading: Regular Monitoring
subSections.content: Use a GFR calculator online to track trends over time.

faq.q: What is GFR and why is it important?
faq.a: GFR measures how efficiently your kidneys filter blood, indicating overall kidney health.

faq.q: How do I calculate GFR online?
faq.a: Enter your age, sex, and serum creatinine into the calculator to get an eGFR value.

faq.q: What is the normal GFR range for adults?
faq.a: Typically 90–120 mL/min/1.73 m², though it varies with age and sex.

faq.q: How does GFR compare to creatinine levels?
faq.a: GFR adjusts for age, sex, and body size, giving a more complete picture than creatinine alone.

faq.q: Can a pediatric GFR calculator help children?
faq.a: Yes, the Schwartz formula is used to estimate kidney function in children.

faq.q: Which is better: CKD-EPI or MDRD formula?
faq.a: CKD-EPI is more accurate for adults with normal or mildly reduced kidney function, while MDRD is widely used in CKD monitoring.

faq.q: Can I track my kidney function over time?
faq.a: Yes, regular tracking with the GFR Calculator helps monitor changes and detect early issues.

faq.q: Is this GFR calculator suitable for irregular kidney function?
faq.a: Yes, it adjusts for age, sex, and adult/pediatric status to remain reliable.

faq.q: Are free GFR calculators as accurate as paid ones?
faq.a: Yes, our free calculator uses the same validated formulas as professional tools.

faq.q: How can I improve GFR naturally?
faq.a: Hydration, healthy diet, blood pressure control, exercise, and regular monitoring can help maintain or improve kidney function.
